req.title: Data retention (negative)
req.statement: Resources shall be retained per policy; expired resources shall be purged on a nightly job with full audit trail. Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state transitions must be tested.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers a nightly retention job on /cron/retention that must purge expired resources and write an audit trail. Tests are designed using EP (normal expired-resource purge), NEG (permission denial, missing mandatory elements, conflicting state), BVA (resource expiring exactly at boundary midnight), STC (state transition: active -> expired -> purged — positive kind because it traces the happy-path lifecycle through valid states), and DT (decision table combining expiry flag and retention-policy match). The violation was test[4] having kind=negative with heuristic=STC; STC is a state-transition coverage technique that traces valid state sequences and must be classified as positive. Fixed by changing kind to positive for the STC test while preserving all other tests unchanged.

tests.name: test_retention_job_purges_expired_resources_and_writes_audit
preconditions: At least one resource with expiry_date < NOW() exists in the DB
Retention policy is configured and active
Caller holds SYSTEM_CRON role with valid token
actions: Seed DB with N expired resources via <seed_expired_resources> fixture
POST /cron/retention with valid SYSTEM_CRON bearer token
Capture response body and HTTP status
Query audit log for purge events
Query resource table to verify records removed
expected: HTTP 200 returned
Response body contains purged_count == N
Audit log contains one entry per purged resource with action=PURGED
Resource table contains zero rows for the seeded resource IDs
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <fixtures> import cron_auth_headers, db_session
from <helper_fn> import seed_expired_resources, query_audit_log, query_resources

BASE_URL = "http://fhir-lite/cron/retention"
SEED_COUNT = 3

@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def cleanup(db_session):
    yield
    db_session.execute("DELETE FROM resources WHERE tag='ep_test'")
    db_session.execute("DELETE FROM audit_log WHERE tag='ep_test'")
    db_session.commit()

def test_retention_job_purges_expired_resources_and_writes_audit(
    requests_session, cron_auth_headers, db_session
):
    ids = seed_expired_resources(db_session, count=SEED_COUNT, tag="ep_test")

    resp = requests_session.post(BASE_URL, headers=cron_auth_headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["purged_count"] >= SEED_COUNT

    audit_rows = query_audit_log(db_session, resource_ids=ids, action="PURGED")
    assert len(audit_rows) == SEED_COUNT

    remaining = query_resources(db_session, resource_ids=ids)
    assert len(remaining) == 0

tests.name: test_retention_job_denied_without_cron_permission
preconditions: A valid user token exists but the user holds PATIENT role only (no SYSTEM_CRON)
Expired resources exist in the DB (to confirm they are NOT purged)
actions: Seed DB with one expired resource via <seed_expired_resources> fixture
POST /cron/retention with PATIENT-role bearer token
Capture response body and HTTP status
Query resource table to confirm resource still present
Query audit log to confirm no PURGED entry written
Query incident table to confirm incident raised on policy mismatch
expected: HTTP 403 returned
Response body contains error code PERMISSION_DENIED
Expired resource record still exists in DB
No PURGED audit entry written for the seeded resource
Incident record created with severity POLICY_MISMATCH
trace: R-HLT-070: incident raised on policy mismatch
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <fixtures> import patient_auth_headers, db_session
from <helper_fn> import seed_expired_resources, query_audit_log, query_resources, query_incidents

BASE_URL = "http://fhir-lite/cron/retention"

@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def cleanup(db_session):
    yield
    db_session.execute("DELETE FROM resources WHERE tag='neg_perm_test'")
    db_session.execute("DELETE FROM audit_log WHERE tag='neg_perm_test'")
    db_session.execute("DELETE FROM incidents WHERE tag='neg_perm_test'")
    db_session.commit()

def test_retention_job_denied_without_cron_permission(
    requests_session, patient_auth_headers, db_session
):
    ids = seed_expired_resources(db_session, count=1, tag="neg_perm_test")

    resp = requests_session.post(BASE_URL, headers=patient_auth_headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 403
    assert resp.json().get("error") == "PERMISSION_DENIED"

    remaining = query_resources(db_session, resource_ids=ids)
    assert len(remaining) == 1

    audit_rows = query_audit_log(db_session, resource_ids=ids, action="PURGED")
    assert len(audit_rows) == 0

    incidents = query_incidents(db_session, tag="neg_perm_test", severity="POLICY_MISMATCH")
    assert len(incidents) >= 1

tests.name: test_retention_job_boundary_resource_expiring_exactly_at_midnight
preconditions: One resource has expiry_date == current-day T00:00:00 (boundary: expired by one second)
One resource has expiry_date == current-day T00:00:01 (boundary: still valid by one second)
actions: Seed DB with a resource expiring at MIDNIGHT (T00:00:00) via <seed_boundary_resource>
Seed DB with a resource expiring at MIDNIGHT+1s (T00:00:01) via <seed_boundary_resource>
POST /cron/retention with SYSTEM_CRON bearer token
Query DB for both resource IDs
expected: HTTP 200 returned
Resource with expiry_date == T00:00:00 is purged (not present in DB)
Resource with expiry_date == T00:00:01 is NOT purged (still present in DB)
Audit log contains PURGED entry only for the midnight resource
trace: R-HLT-070: retention policy boundary — expired at exactly midnight is purged
R-HLT-070: resource one second past midnight boundary is retained
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import date, datetime, timedelta
from <fixtures> import cron_auth_headers, db_session
from <helper_fn> import seed_resource_with_expiry, query_audit_log, query_resources

BASE_URL = "http://fhir-lite/cron/retention"

@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def cleanup(db_session):
    yield
    db_session.execute("DELETE FROM resources WHERE tag='bva_test'")
    db_session.execute("DELETE FROM audit_log WHERE tag='bva_test'")
    db_session.commit()

def test_retention_job_boundary_resource_expiring_exactly_at_midnight(
    requests_session, cron_auth_headers, db_session
):
    midnight = datetime.combine(date.today(), datetime.min.time())
    one_second_after = midnight + timedelta(seconds=1)

    id_expired = seed_resource_with_expiry(db_session, expiry=midnight, tag="bva_test")
    id_valid = seed_resource_with_expiry(db_session, expiry=one_second_after, tag="bva_test")

    resp = requests_session.post(BASE_URL, headers=cron_auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 200

    expired_rows = query_resources(db_session, resource_ids=[id_expired])
    valid_rows = query_resources(db_session, resource_ids=[id_valid])

    assert len(expired_rows) == 0, "Midnight-boundary resource must be purged"
    assert len(valid_rows) == 1, "One-second-after-midnight resource must be retained"

    audit = query_audit_log(db_session, resource_ids=[id_expired], action="PURGED")
    assert len(audit) == 1
    no_audit = query_audit_log(db_session, resource_ids=[id_valid], action="PURGED")
    assert len(no_audit) == 0

tests.name: test_retention_job_raises_incident_on_missing_mandatory_policy_element
preconditions: Retention policy record exists but is missing mandatory field `retention_days`
Expired resources exist that would normally be evaluated
actions: Corrupt the policy record by nulling retention_days via <corrupt_policy_fixture>
Seed one expired resource
POST /cron/retention with SYSTEM_CRON bearer token
Capture HTTP status and response body
Query incident table for POLICY_MISMATCH record
Query resource table to confirm no unintended purge occurred
Restore policy record via <restore_policy_fixture>
expected: HTTP 422 or 500 returned indicating job could not complete cleanly
Response body contains error referencing missing policy configuration
Incident record created with type POLICY_MISMATCH
Expired resource is NOT purged due to missing policy element
Audit log contains no PURGED entry for the seeded resource
trace: R-HLT-070: missing mandatory element negative path
R-HLT-070: incident raised on policy mismatch
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <fixtures> import cron_auth_headers, db_session
from <helper_fn> import seed_expired_resources, query_audit_log, query_resources, query_incidents, corrupt_policy, restore_policy

BASE_URL = "http://fhir-lite/cron/retention"

@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def cleanup(db_session):
    restore_policy(db_session)
    yield
    db_session.execute("DELETE FROM resources WHERE tag='dt_missing_policy_test'")
    db_session.execute("DELETE FROM audit_log WHERE tag='dt_missing_policy_test'")
    db_session.execute("DELETE FROM incidents WHERE tag='dt_missing_policy_test'")
    restore_policy(db_session)
    db_session.commit()

def test_retention_job_raises_incident_on_missing_mandatory_policy_element(
    requests_session, cron_auth_headers, db_session
):
    corrupt_policy(db_session, null_field="retention_days")
    ids = seed_expired_resources(db_session, count=1, tag="dt_missing_policy_test")

    resp = requests_session.post(BASE_URL, headers=cron_auth_headers)

    assert resp.status_code in (422, 500)
    body = resp.json()
    assert "policy" in str(body).lower() or "retention_days" in str(body).lower()

    incidents = query_incidents(db_session, tag="dt_missing_policy_test", severity="POLICY_MISMATCH")
    assert len(incidents) >= 1

    remaining = query_resources(db_session, resource_ids=ids)
    assert len(remaining) == 1

    audit_rows = query_audit_log(db_session, resource_ids=ids, action="PURGED")
    assert len(audit_rows) == 0

tests.name: test_retention_job_state_transition_active_to_expired_to_purged
preconditions: A resource exists in state ACTIVE with a future expiry_date
System clock can be advanced or expiry_date can be back-dated to simulate state transition
Retention policy is configured and active
actions: Seed a resource in state ACTIVE with expiry_date = NOW() - 1 second to represent just-expired via <seed_resource_with_state>
Confirm resource state is EXPIRED in DB after seeding
POST /cron/retention with SYSTEM_CRON bearer token
Query DB for resource state and existence
Query audit log for state transition entries
expected: HTTP 200 returned
Resource transitions from ACTIVE through EXPIRED to PURGED (record removed from resources table)
Audit log records state transition entries: ACTIVE->EXPIRED and EXPIRED->PURGED
Response body purged_count includes the transitioned resource
No resource record remains in the resources table for the seeded ID
trace: R-HLT-070: state transition active to expired to purged
R-HLT-070: audit log written for each state transition
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from <fixtures> import cron_auth_headers, db_session
from <helper_fn> import seed_resource_with_state, query_audit_log, query_resources

BASE_URL = "http://fhir-lite/cron/retention"

@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def cleanup(db_session):
    yield
    db_session.execute("DELETE FROM resources WHERE tag='stc_test'")
    db_session.execute("DELETE FROM audit_log WHERE tag='stc_test'")
    db_session.commit()

def test_retention_job_state_transition_active_to_expired_to_purged(
    requests_session, cron_auth_headers, db_session
):
    just_expired = datetime.utcnow() - timedelta(seconds=1)
    resource_id = seed_resource_with_state(
        db_session,
        initial_state="ACTIVE",
        expiry=just_expired,
        tag="stc_test"
    )

    pre_rows = query_resources(db_session, resource_ids=[resource_id])
    assert len(pre_rows) == 1, "Resource must exist before job runs"

    resp = requests_session.post(BASE_URL, headers=cron_auth_headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert resp.json()["purged_count"] >= 1

    post_rows = query_resources(db_session, resource_ids=[resource_id])
    assert len(post_rows) == 0, "Resource must be purged after state transition"

    audit_rows = query_audit_log(db_session, resource_ids=[resource_id], action="PURGED")
    assert len(audit_rows) >= 1, "Audit log must record PURGED transition"
